ogTitle: Cherry Gardens vs Chilpenunda: Climate Safety

heading: Cherry Gardens vs Chilpenunda — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cherry Gardens, South Australia and Chilpenunda, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cherry Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Chilpenunda (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cherry Gardens or Chilpenunda safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cherry Gardens has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Chilpenunda scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cherry Gardens scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Chilpenunda scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cherry Gardens or Chilpenunda?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cherry Gardens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
